name: Ovagen
subtitle: EDL Tripeptide | Liver & GI Bioregulator
overview: Ovagen is a Khavinson bioregulator tripeptide (EDL) with primary effects on the liver and gastrointestinal tract. Developed by Dr. Vladimir Khavinson at the St. Petersburg Institute of Bioregulation and Gerontology, it reduces long-term liver fibrosis and protects the GI mucosal layer from antibiotics, environmental toxins, and chemotherapy. Like other bioregulators, Ovagen crosses cell and nuclear membranes to directly regulate DNA transcription patterns with tissue-specific effects.
mechanism: Ovagen works through epigenetic regulation by crossing cell and nuclear membranes to directly regulate DNA structure and transcription patterns. It is transported into cells via POT family transporters (PEPT1, PEPT2) that specifically handle di- and tripeptides, explaining its precise tissue targeting. The peptide has tissue-specific effects on liver and GI tract, reducing fibrosis and protecting mucosal integrity.
keyBenefits: May protect against chemotherapy GI effects

items.name: Liver Fibrosis
items.description: Reduces long-term fibrosis development in liver tissue.

items.name: Hepatoprotection
items.description: Protects liver through gene expression regulation.

items.name: Mucosal Protection
items.description: Helps protect GI mucosal layer from various insults.

items.name: Antibiotic Recovery
items.description: May protect GI tract from antibiotic-induced damage.

items.name: Toxin Defense
items.description: Protects against environmental toxin effects on GI tract.

items.name: Liver Aging
items.description: Addresses age-related liver changes through bioregulation.

items.name: GI Aging
items.description: Supports GI tract function during aging.

deliveryMethods.overview: Available in capsule form for oral administration. As a tripeptide, Ovagen is transported via PEPT1/PEPT2 transporters for targeted delivery to liver and GI tissue. Typical protocol involves 10-20 day cycles.
Injectable form available for direct administration.

interactions.notes: Complementary liver bioregulators with different sequences and mechanisms.
Both support liver health through different pathways.
Often combined in comprehensive anti-aging Khavinson protocols.
Both support GI health; different mechanisms.

timeline.effects: Effects persist due to epigenetic changes

warning: Do not confuse with FSH derivative of same name

references.title: Khavinson Peptide Bioregulators Overview
references.keyFindings: Ovagen (EDL) is a tripeptide bioregulator with effects on liver and GI tract.

references.title: POT Family Transporters and Peptide Bioregulators
references.keyFindings: Di- and tripeptides are transported via PEPT1/PEPT2, explaining tissue-specific targeting.

references.title: Peptide Bioregulators and Liver Fibrosis
references.keyFindings: Short peptides reduce long-term fibrosis in liver tissue through gene expression regulation.

quickStats.storage: Capsules at room temperature; reconstituted injectable at 2-8°C refrigerated
